research_question: Does a model-generated title-artist catalog mismatch remain a known-relation error after requiring independent candidate-free recovery, and is relation validity decodable at title end before artist generation under entity-disjoint evaluation?

discovery_contexts.profile: likes melodic synth-pop, coldwave, electronic pop, and polished but unusual production
discovery_contexts.current_need: discovering real synth-pop deep cuts from the 1980s through the 2010s while avoiding the artists' best-known singles

discovery_contexts.profile: likes Japanese city pop, boogie, fusion, and warm analog production
discovery_contexts.current_need: a late-night city-pop set with genuine catalog tracks beyond the most internationally famous songs

discovery_contexts.profile: likes post-punk, art pop, new wave, and tense rhythmic arrangements
discovery_contexts.current_need: less obvious real tracks that connect post-punk guitars with art-pop songwriting across different decades

discovery_contexts.profile: likes ambient, minimal electronic music, drone, and patient instrumental development
discovery_contexts.current_need: real long-form ambient or minimal tracks for focused listening, avoiding generic relaxation compilations

discovery_contexts.profile: likes underground hip-hop, sample-based production, abstract beats, and distinctive flows
discovery_contexts.current_need: real album tracks with memorable production, naming the recording artist rather than only the producer

discovery_contexts.profile: likes alternative R&B, electronic soul, intimate vocals, and unusual collaborations
discovery_contexts.current_need: real collaborative or featured-vocal tracks where the exact credited recording artist matters

discovery_contexts.profile: likes modern jazz, small ensembles, restrained improvisation, and detailed rhythm sections
discovery_contexts.current_need: real twenty-first-century jazz recordings led by distinct bandleaders, without relying on standards compilations

discovery_contexts.profile: likes folk, singer-songwriter albums, intimate acoustic arrangements, and narrative lyrics
discovery_contexts.current_need: real album cuts from several decades, avoiding each artist's most obvious signature song

discovery_contexts.profile: likes IDM, electro, glitch, and experimental electronic labels
discovery_contexts.current_need: real catalog tracks with complex rhythm and unusual timbre, correctly separating track artist from label or compilation name

discovery_contexts.profile: likes film and game soundtracks, dream pop, orchestral pop, and atmospheric songs
discovery_contexts.current_need: real vocal songs associated with films or games, naming the recording performer rather than the composer or fictional character

discovery_contexts.profile: likes Latin alternative, electronic cumbia, art rock, and regional crossovers
discovery_contexts.current_need: real Spanish- or Portuguese-language tracks beyond global crossover hits, with exact artist credits

discovery_contexts.profile: likes progressive metal, doom, post-metal, and technically detailed heavy music
discovery_contexts.current_need: real deep cuts that distinguish adjacent heavy subgenres and do not confuse similarly named bands

holdout_contexts.current_need: a calm night drive with steady momentum and no loud drops

holdout_contexts.profile: likes emotionally direct pop, alternative R&B, and melodic songwriting
holdout_contexts.current_need: songs that move from grief toward resolve without becoming triumphant too quickly

holdout_contexts.profile: likes hip-hop, industrial electronic music, metal, and forceful percussion
holdout_contexts.current_need: controlled high-energy music for heavy strength training

holdout_contexts.profile: likes jazz, soul, bossa nova, and unobtrusive arrangements
holdout_contexts.current_need: warm dinner music with moderate tempo and no showy solos

holdout_contexts.profile: likes trip-hop, mellow jazz, acoustic soul, and understated vocals
holdout_contexts.current_need: reading alone in a rainy cafe with a soft pulse

holdout_contexts.profile: likes bright indie pop, funk, pop-rock, and strong hooks
holdout_contexts.current_need: an open-road summer drive with forward motion and no melancholy ballads

holdout_contexts.profile: likes modern classical, solo piano, chamber music, and minimalism
holdout_contexts.current_need: deep work with real instrumental recordings and very low distraction

holdout_contexts.profile: likes disco, house, funk, and exuberant vocal dance music

pilot_continuation_gate.rationale: Eight recoverable conflicts among approximately 360 pilot events are the minimum yield consistent with reaching about 30 at the registered 1440-event discovery cap.

knowledge_recoverability.reference_semantics: shared catalog-supported artist, not asserted unique worldwide

disjointness: normalized emitted and reference artist disjoint

required_before_intervention: holdout pre-artist hidden-state AUROC exceeds the best frozen surface baseline
cluster-bootstrap 95% lower bound for AUROC difference exceeds zero
direction is consistent across at least two frozen generation seeds
no entity leakage or split violation
mechanism_gate.intervention_primary_outcome: correct-reference minus emitted-artist sequence logprob margin

claim_boundaries: This is a relation-stress study and cannot estimate ordinary product hallucination prevalence.
Catalog conflict alone is not evidence that the model knew a song relation.
Probe decodability is not subjective awareness or evidence of deception.
The title-end position is pre-artist and pre-relation-completion, not globally pre-output.
No intervention runs unless the frozen held-out probe gate passes.
Product deployment should still prefer retrieval, IDs, constrained decoding, or catalog validation.
